thesis: First $1B quarterly revenue (+32% YoY) in Q1 2026; EPS beat $0.60 vs $0.51 expected (18% surprise); raised full-year guidance. AI and cloud monitoring infrastructure is mission-critical and expanding. Multiple analysts raised PTs to $215-225 post-earnings (Guggenheim, Evercore, Wedbush, Barclays). Strong Buy consensus, avg PT ~$205. GS outlier SELL at $139.

Rezdiffra is the only approved MASH/NASH treatment; $1.1B+ TTM net sales, Q1 2026 revenue +127% YoY to $311M; 42,250+ patients on drug with April 2026 being strongest new-Rx month since launch. Strong Buy consensus, avg PT $671-709 (Truist raised to $709 on May 13) implying 33-40% upside. Blockbuster trajectory with large underserved patient population.

Strait of Hormuz still disrupted despite April 7 ceasefire (violence flaring May 5-8, Brent crude ~$105-107); Berkshire Hathaway backing; $9.7B OxyChem sale used for debt reduction to $15B target; Q1 EPS beat at $1.06; production 1.43M BOE/day; mean analyst PT $64 (14% upside). Energy structural tailwind while Hormuz remains restricted.

Global payment network dominance (4.9B credentials); Q2 FY2026 EPS +20% YoY ($3.31 vs $3.10 expected), revenue +17% YoY ($11.23B vs $10.75B expected); $20B buyback approved, quarterly dividend raised to $0.67; Agentic Commerce expansion (AI-initiated payments). Wide-moat business resilient across economic cycles.
